command: באג: כאשר הבעלים שולח הודעה למישהו מהטלפון שלו (לא דרך הממשק), ההודעה מתווספת לשיחות אבל השם של אותו אדם נשמר כשם של הוואטסאפ של הבעלים במקום כשם של המקבל. צריך לבדוק בקוד של session-manager איפה נשמר contact_name כשמגיעה הודעה יוצאת מהבעלים, ולתקן כך שה-contact_name יהיה שם האיש שאליו שלחו ולא שם הבעלים

plan.summary: Fix contact name bug in session-manager where outgoing owner messages incorrectly save the owner's WhatsApp name as the contact name instead of the recipient's name.

tasks.instruction: Inspect session-manager/src/message-handler.ts to locate where contact_name is set for outgoing messages (isFromMe: true). Identify the bug: likely using the owner's WhatsApp name (pushName) instead of the recipient's contact name. Fix by ensuring contact_name is set to the recipient's name (priority: phonebook > push > phone) when saving to conversations table.
tasks.context: WhatsApp contact name priority: phonebook (contact.name) > push name (contact.notify) > phone number. Outgoing messages trigger messages.upsert with isFromMe: true. Current code may incorrectly set contact_name from the sender (owner) instead of recipient. Check handleIncomingMessage function and any related logic for outgoing message handling.

tasks.instruction: Design test cases to verify the fix: 1) Simulate outgoing owner message via WhatsApp (not UI) and check that contact_name in conversations table matches recipient's name. 2) Test with various recipient name sources (phonebook, push name, phone only). 3) Verify no regression for incoming messages. Provide specific steps and expected outcomes.
tasks.context: Bug occurs when owner sends a message directly from their phone. Focus on session-manager logic. Use existing test data or mock scenarios. Ensure multi-tenant isolation is maintained.

tasks.instruction: Review the fix for security implications: ensure no data leakage between tenants (tenant_id is correctly used), contact_name updates don't bypass RLS, and input validation is maintained. Confirm the fix doesn't introduce SQL injection or other vulnerabilities.
tasks.context: session-manager uses Supabase service role key. Check that contact_name updates are scoped to the correct tenant and conversation. Verify no unauthorized access to contact data.

results.output: [System Error]: Reached maximum number of tool iterations. Exiting early.
[System Error]: Reached maximum number of tool iterations. Exiting early.
[System Error]: Reached maximum number of tool iterations. Exiting early.

synthesis.implementation_plan: 1. Navigate to session-manager codebase and locate the message handling module (likely in src/handlers/message_handler.js or similar). 2. Identify the function that processes outgoing messages from the owner (search for 'outgoing', 'owner', 'sendMessage'). 3. Examine the logic where contact_name is set when a message is sent from the owner's phone (not via interface). Look for a variable assignment like contact_name = owner.whatsapp_name. 4. Modify the code to set contact_name to the recipient's name instead. This may involve: a. Extracting recipient details from the message payload (e.g., message.recipient.name). b. Updating the database or session state accordingly. 5. Update any related tests in test/message_handler.test.js to verify the fix. 6. Run existing tests to ensure no regressions.
blockers: Clarity on message payload structure for recipient information
synthesis.pm_notes: Coordinate with backend team to understand the exact message flow and data structure. Ensure QA validates the fix across different message scenarios. Security review may be needed if contact name handling involves sensitive data.
